name: Fintech & Banking

users.context: Reviews transaction-monitoring alerts, investigates suspicious activity, files SARs/STRs, and clears or escalates cases. Queue-driven, evidence-heavy, accountable to regulators.
users.expertise: Expert in AML/CFT typologies and sanctions; needs full investigative context and immutable case notes.

users.context: Verifies new customer identity, screens against sanctions/PEP/adverse-media, assesses risk rating, and approves or rejects account opening. Balances fraud prevention against onboarding friction.
users.expertise: Expert in identity verification and CDD/EDD; works through document checks and watchlist hits.

users.context: Opens accounts, transfers money, pays bills, applies for credit, and disputes transactions via mobile/web app. Expects instant, trustworthy, secure interactions; anxious about money and fraud.
users.expertise: Novice to intermediate; assumes nothing technical, highly sensitive to errors involving their money.

users.context: Monitors real-time fraud signals, reviews flagged transactions, places holds, and contacts customers. Speed-critical: false declines anger customers, missed fraud loses money.
users.expertise: Expert in fraud patterns and risk scoring; needs low-latency decisioning tools.

users.context: Advises clients, executes/approves trades and transfers, ensures suitability and disclosures. Fiduciary duty and record-keeping obligations.
users.expertise: Expert in products and suitability rules; needs client 360 and compliant communication logging.

users.context: Monitors liquidity, exposure, and limits; approves large transfers and settlements. Real-time positions with hard cut-offs.
users.expertise: Expert in market/credit/liquidity risk and limit frameworks.

workflows.name: KYC / customer onboarding and CDD
steps: Screen against sanctions, PEP, and adverse media
Assign risk rating and apply EDD if high-risk
Approve, reject, or request more information

workflows.name: Transaction monitoring and SAR/STR filing
steps: Ingest transactions and run monitoring rules/models
Investigate with customer and transaction context

workflows.name: Money transfer / payment initiation
steps: Validate balance, limits, and beneficiary details

workflows.name: Transaction dispute / chargeback handling
steps: Customer raises dispute on a transaction
Provisionally credit if required by regulation
Notify customer with outcome and rights

workflows.name: Credit / loan application and decisioning
steps: Pull credit bureau and affordability data

risks: Approving an account that should have failed KYC/sanctions screening (regulatory breach)
Missing or late SAR/STR filing exposing the firm to enforcement penalties
Money sent to the wrong beneficiary or amount with no easy reversal
False fraud decline blocking a legitimate customer; or missed fraud causing loss
Exposing account/balance data to the wrong customer (authorization/IDOR failure)
Sanctions/PEP match wrongly auto-cleared, processing a prohibited payment
Inadequate strong customer authentication enabling account takeover
Incorrect interest/fee calculation or rounding error at scale
Unexplainable automated credit decision violating fair-lending/adverse-action rules
Breaching a treasury/exposure limit due to stale real-time position data
environment_constraints: Real-time, low-latency decisioning with hard settlement cut-offs
High-trust, money-on-the-line context where errors are unforgiving
Heavy regulatory examination and reporting deadlines
Adversarial: active fraud and social-engineering attempts
Mobile-first consumer apps on untrusted devices and networks
Strong authentication friction must be balanced against UX
domain_patterns: Immutable audit trail and case notes
Confirmation review screen before irreversible money movement
audit_compliance: AML/CFT, BSA, and FATF recommendations (SAR/STR, CDD/EDD)
SOX financial controls for public institutions
Fair-lending / adverse-action (e.g., ECOA) and SOC 2 for service providers
accessibility_concerns: Money amounts and account numbers must be unambiguous and screen-reader friendly
Critical confirmations must not rely on colour (e.g., success/error) alone
Authentication flows (OTP, biometrics) need accessible fallbacks
Time-limited steps (OTP entry) need adequate, adjustable timeouts
Clear, jargon-free language for financial decisions and disclosures
High contrast and large targets for stressed users completing money transfers
anti_patterns: Frictionless irreversible transfer with no clear review/confirmation step
Auto-clearing sanctions/PEP hits without analyst review
Exposing object IDs allowing access to another customer's account (IDOR)
Colour-only encoding of transaction success/decline or risk
Aggressive session timeouts that drop a transfer mid-flow
Opaque declines with no reason (fails adverse-action requirements)
Storing or displaying full card/PAN data unnecessarily
Editable case notes that destroy the immutable investigative trail
